req.title: Manager views onboarding-task
req.statement: An authenticated manager shall be able to view a onboarding-task through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

doc.reasoning: R-WEB-028 requires an authenticated manager to view an onboarding-task via the UI, with mandatory field validation and audit record creation on success. I derived: (1) a positive EP test confirming a valid manager can load an existing task and an audit record is written; (2) a NEG test confirming an unauthenticated user receives a 401/redirect; (3) a NEG/DT test confirming a user with a non-manager role receives a 403; (4) a BVA test exercising the boundary of viewing a task whose ID is exactly at the minimum valid integer (1); (5) a STC test confirming the view action transitions the audit log from N to N+1 entries. Together these cover EP, NEG, DT, BVA, and STC heuristics as required.

tests.name: test_manager_can_view_onboarding_task_successfully
preconditions: At least one onboarding-task record exists with known id
actions: Obtain manager auth token via <get_auth_token> fixture
Retrieve an existing task id via <seed_onboarding_task> fixture
expected: Task detail form is visible with correct task id
Audit record for VIEW action is written to audit log
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

def test_manager_can_view_onboarding_task_successfully(
    manager_page,           # <fixture>: authenticated Playwright page as manager
    seed_onboarding_task,   # <fixture>: creates task, yields task_id, teardown deletes
    audit_log_client,       # <fixture>: REST client for audit API
    base_url,               # <fixture>: e.g. "http://hr-app"
):
    task_id = seed_onboarding_task
    pre_count = audit_log_client.get_view_count(task_id)

    response = manager_page.goto(f"{base_url}/onboarding-tasks/{task_id}")
    assert response.status == 200

    expect(manager_page.locator("[data-testid='task-detail-form']")).to_be_visible()
    expect(manager_page.locator("[data-testid='task-id']")).to_have_text(str(task_id))

    post_count = audit_log_client.get_view_count(task_id)
    assert post_count == pre_count + 1, "Audit record not written after view"

tests.name: test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_view_onboarding_task
preconditions: No authentication session exists (unauthenticated browser context)
At least one onboarding-task record exists with known id
actions: Open a fresh browser context with no credentials via <anon_page> fixture
expected: HTTP response status is 401 OR page redirects to login
Task detail form is NOT visible
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

def test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_view_onboarding_task(
    anon_page,              # <fixture>: Playwright page with no session
    seed_onboarding_task,   # <fixture>: creates task, yields task_id
    audit_log_client,       # <fixture>: REST client for audit API
    base_url,
    login_url,              # <fixture>: e.g. "http://hr-app/login"
):
    task_id = seed_onboarding_task
    pre_count = audit_log_client.get_view_count(task_id)

    response = anon_page.goto(f"{base_url}/onboarding-tasks/{task_id}")

    is_401 = response is not None and response.status == 401
    is_redirected = anon_page.url.startswith(login_url)
    assert is_401 or is_redirected, (
        f"Expected 401 or login redirect, got status={getattr(response,'status',None)} "
        f"url={anon_page.url}"
    )

    expect(anon_page.locator("[data-testid='task-detail-form']")).not_to_be_visible()

    post_count = audit_log_client.get_view_count(task_id)
    assert post_count == pre_count, "Audit record must not be written for unauthenticated access"

tests.name: test_non_manager_role_forbidden_from_viewing_onboarding_task
preconditions: User is authenticated but has role 'employee' (not manager)
At least one onboarding-task record exists with known id
actions: Obtain auth session for employee-role user via <employee_page> fixture
expected: HTTP response status is 403 OR forbidden error page is shown
Task detail form is NOT visible
trace: R-WEB-028: error pathway 403 if role forbidden
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

def test_non_manager_role_forbidden_from_viewing_onboarding_task(
    employee_page,          # <fixture>: authenticated Playwright page as employee
    seed_onboarding_task,   # <fixture>: creates task, yields task_id
    audit_log_client,       # <fixture>: REST client for audit API
    base_url,
):
    task_id = seed_onboarding_task
    pre_count = audit_log_client.get_view_count(task_id)

    response = employee_page.goto(f"{base_url}/onboarding-tasks/{task_id}")

    is_403 = response is not None and response.status == 403
    has_forbidden_indicator = employee_page.locator(
        "[data-testid='forbidden-message']"
    ).is_visible()
    assert is_403 or has_forbidden_indicator, (
        f"Expected 403 or forbidden UI, got status={getattr(response,'status',None)}"
    )

    expect(employee_page.locator("[data-testid='task-detail-form']")).not_to_be_visible()

    post_count = audit_log_client.get_view_count(task_id)
    assert post_count == pre_count, "Audit record must not be written for forbidden access"

tests.name: test_manager_views_onboarding_task_with_minimum_boundary_id
preconditions: An onboarding-task record with the minimum valid ID (1) exists
actions: Obtain manager auth token via <manager_page> fixture
expected: Task detail form renders with id=1
Audit record for VIEW action on task id=1 is written
trace: R-WEB-028: BVA on task id lower boundary (id=1)
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

MIN_TASK_ID = 1

def test_manager_views_onboarding_task_with_minimum_boundary_id(
    manager_page,           # <fixture>: authenticated Playwright page as manager
    ensure_task_id_exists,  # <fixture>: ensures task with given id exists, idempotent
    audit_log_client,       # <fixture>: REST client for audit API
    base_url,
):
    ensure_task_id_exists(MIN_TASK_ID)
    pre_count = audit_log_client.get_view_count(MIN_TASK_ID)

    response = manager_page.goto(f"{base_url}/onboarding-tasks/{MIN_TASK_ID}")
    assert response.status == 200, f"Expected 200, got {response.status}"

    expect(manager_page.locator("[data-testid='task-detail-form']")).to_be_visible()
    expect(manager_page.locator("[data-testid='task-id']")).to_have_text(str(MIN_TASK_ID))

    post_count = audit_log_client.get_view_count(MIN_TASK_ID)
    assert post_count == pre_count + 1, (
        f"Audit count should increment by 1: pre={pre_count} post={post_count}"
    )

tests.name: test_audit_log_transitions_on_manager_view_action
preconditions: An onboarding-task record exists (state: UNVIEWED in audit context)
Audit log count for the task is known before the action
actions: Record pre-view audit count for the task
Record post-view audit count for the task
expected: State transitions from UNVIEWED_IN_SESSION to VIEWED_IN_SESSION
Audit log count increments by exactly 1
Latest audit entry has action=VIEW, actor=manager, entity_id=task_id
trace: R-WEB-028: audit record written on success
R-WEB-028: STC from pre-view to post-view state with single audit entry
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

def test_audit_log_transitions_on_manager_view_action(
    manager_page,           # <fixture>: authenticated Playwright page as manager
    seed_onboarding_task,   # <fixture>: creates task, yields task_id
    audit_log_client,       # <fixture>: REST client for audit API
    manager_username,       # <fixture>: str username of the manager
    base_url,
):
    task_id = seed_onboarding_task

    pre_count = audit_log_client.get_view_count(task_id)

    response = manager_page.goto(f"{base_url}/onboarding-tasks/{task_id}")
    assert response.status == 200

    post_count = audit_log_client.get_view_count(task_id)
    assert post_count == pre_count + 1, (
        f"Expected exactly 1 new audit entry; pre={pre_count} post={post_count}"
    )

    latest = audit_log_client.get_latest_entry(task_id)
    assert latest["action"] == "VIEW"
    assert latest["entity_id"] == str(task_id)
    assert latest["actor"] == manager_username
